chapter_title.en: What a Person Should Say Upon Waking From Sleep

text: 1389 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ اَلْحَجَّاجِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَنَّهُ كَانَ إِذَا قَامَ آخِرَ اَللَّيْلِ رَفَعَ صَوْتَهُ حَتَّى يَسْمَعَ أَهْلُ اَلدَّارِ وَ يَقُولُ: اَللَّهُمَّ أَعِنِّي عَلَى هَوْلِ اَلْمُطَّلَعِ وَ وَسِّعْ عَلَيَّ اَلْمَضْجَعَ وَ اُرْزُقْنِي خَيْرَ مَا قَبْلَ اَلْمَوْتِ وَ اُرْزُقْنِي خَيْرَ مَا بَعْدَ اَلْمَوْتِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1389 - Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj reports from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that when he would rise in the last part of the night, he would raise his voice such that the people of the household could hear and say:
Allahumma a‘inni ‘ala hawli al-muttala‘, wa wassi‘ ‘alayya al-madja‘, wa urzuqni khayra ma qabl al-mawt, wa urzuqni khayra ma ba‘d al-mawt
"O’ Allah (swt), help me with the terror of the Rising (on the Day of Judgment), make my resting place spacious, grant me the best of what comes before death, and grant me the best of what comes after death."
